headline: An actively exploited Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day (CVE-2026-35273) is letting ShinyHunters steal gigabytes of data from hundreds of organizations, with American universities hit hardest.
emerging_trends: AI is simultaneously a weapon and a target: Google is suing a China-based network for abusing Gemini in phishing, while new Agentjacking and LangGraph attacks turn AI coding agents and frameworks into remote-code-execution surfaces.
Long-dormant footholds are surfacing en masse, from a decade-old phpBB authentication bypass to Velvet Ant's nearly ten-year backdoor buried inside Linux PAM and OpenSSH login components.
Software and developer supply chains remain a prime entry point, with over 400 Arch Linux AUR packages hijacked to drop an infostealer and eBPF rootkit.
Regulators are escalating breach penalties, with South Korea fining Coupang a record $409 million and a $47 million settlement fund approved for 23andMe victims.
Coordinated law enforcement is dismantling cybercrime infrastructure, taking down the AudiA6 laundering service and Sniper Dz phishing platform and securing a Conti guilty plea.

topics.title: Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day exploited by ShinyHunters
topics.last_24h: Researchers and CISA confirmed active exploitation of CVE-2026-35273 in Oracle PeopleSoft/PeopleTools, with Google attributing data theft from hundreds of organizations to ShinyHunters.
topics.summary: CVE-2026-35273 is a missing-authentication-for-critical-function flaw in Oracle PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools that allows unauthenticated attackers to steal large volumes of data. The ShinyHunters group has weaponized it broadly, disproportionately affecting American universities running the ERP software. Oracle has issued mitigations but has not publicly confirmed in-the-wild exploitation; CISA added the bug to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Organizations running PeopleSoft should apply Oracle's fixes immediately and hunt for signs of bulk data exfiltration.

sources.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed
Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters
CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog

topics.title: Ivanti Sentry flaw under attack, CISA issues 3-day patch order
topics.last_24h: CISA invoked new Binding Operational Directive 26-04 to give federal agencies three days to patch an actively exploited Ivanti Sentry flaw, as exploitation attempts hit honeypots.
topics.summary: A critical OS command injection vulnerability in Ivanti Sentry allows attackers to execute arbitrary code with root privileges. Security researchers are already observing exploitation attempts against internet-facing honeypots, indicating opportunistic scanning and attacks. CISA ordered federal agencies to remediate by Sunday under the newly issued BOD 26-04, which prioritizes patching based on risk. Administrators should patch Sentry immediately and review systems for signs of root-level compromise.

sources.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Ivanti flaw by Sunday
Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots

topics.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR packages hijacked
topics.last_24h: Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository, rewriting build scripts to deploy a Rust infostealer and an eBPF rootkit.
topics.summary: The Arch User Repository (AUR) is Arch Linux's community package collection where build scripts run on users' machines. Attackers compromised over 400 packages and altered their build scripts to install a Rust-based credential stealer that harvests developer secrets and access tokens. When it runs with root, the malware can also load an eBPF rootkit to hide its presence. Arch users who built any AUR packages this week should assume credential compromise, rotate secrets, and inspect systems for the rootkit.

sources.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer

topics.title: AI abused by criminals and exposed as new attack surface
topics.last_24h: Google sued a China-based network for weaponizing Gemini in smishing, while researchers disclosed Agentjacking against AI coding agents and an RCE chain in LangGraph.
topics.summary: Google is pursuing legal action against Outsider Enterprise, a China-based cybercrime network behind the Outsider phishing-as-a-service kit, which abused Gemini to build phishing sites and send scam texts affecting hundreds of thousands of victims. Separately, Tenet Security described Agentjacking, a technique using fake Sentry error reports to trick AI coding agents into running arbitrary code on developer machines. Researchers also disclosed three patched LangGraph flaws, including an SQL-injection-based chain leading to remote code execution in self-hosted AI agents. Teams deploying AI agents should patch LangGraph, scrutinize agent inputs, and govern agent behavior using NIST and ISO frameworks.

sources.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
Agentjacking Attack Tricks AI Coding Agents Into Running Malicious Code
LangGraph Flaw Chain Exposes Self-Hosted AI Agents to Remote Code Execution
Google sues China-based scammers over Gemini AI abuse

topics.title: Law enforcement disrupts cybercrime infrastructure
topics.last_24h: Europol dismantled the AudiA6 crypto-laundering service, INTERPOL took down the Sniper Dz phishing platform, and a Ukrainian national pleaded guilty to Conti ransomware charges.
topics.summary: An international operation dismantled AudiA6, a cryptocurrency laundering service that washed more than €336 million (~$389 million) in illicit funds for ransomware gangs and other criminals between 2022 and 2025. INTERPOL's Operation Ramz disrupted Sniper Dz, a decade-old phishing-as-a-service platform, with 201 arrests across 13 MENA-region countries, including its primary operator. In the US, a Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges tied to the Conti ransomware operation. The coordinated actions cut financial pipelines and operator capacity behind major cybercrime ecosystems.

sources.title: Europol Disrupts AudiA6 Crypto Laundering Service Used by Ransomware Gangs
INTERPOL Operation Takes Down Sniper Dz Phishing Platform, Arrests Administrator
Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation

topics.title: Major breaches and record regulatory fines
topics.last_24h: South Korea fined Coupang a record $409 million over a data breach, a $47 million 23andMe settlement was approved, and Novo Nordisk and France's Tchap disclosed breaches.
topics.summary: South Korea's privacy commission issued its largest-ever data breach penalty, $409 million against e-commerce giant Coupang, surpassing the earlier SK Telecom fine. A bankruptcy administrator approved a $47 million settlement fund for the roughly 7 million 23andMe customers whose genetic data was stolen starting in 2023. Pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk disclosed a breach of clinical trial patient data, and the French government said a breach of its Tchap encrypted messenger exposed over 73,000 public-sector accounts. The cluster underscores rising regulatory costs and continued exposure of sensitive health and government data.

sources.title: South Korea hits Coupang with record $409 million fine over data breach
Bankruptcy admin approves settlement fund of $47 million for 23andMe data breach victims
Pharma giant Novo Nordisk discloses breach of clinical trials data
Over 73,000 French govt employees affected in Tchap messenger breach

topics.title: Velvet Ant backdoored Linux login software for nearly a decade
topics.last_24h: Sygnia disclosed that China-nexus group Velvet Ant hid inside Linux PAM and OpenSSH login components for close to ten years to evade detection.
topics.summary: Rather than hiding on closely monitored endpoints, the China-linked Velvet Ant group backdoored the PAM and OpenSSH components that govern authentication on a targeted network. By embedding access in the Linux login system itself, the attackers placed their foothold where routine cleanup and standard monitoring could not reach. The intrusion persisted for nearly a decade. Defenders should verify the integrity of PAM modules and SSH binaries and treat authentication infrastructure as a high-value detection target.

sources.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade

topics.title: Additional exploited and long-standing vulnerabilities patched
topics.last_24h: WatchTowr released PoC details for an exploited Check Point VPN auth bypass, phpBB fixed a decade-old auth bypass, and Chrome 149 patched 28 flaws.
topics.summary: WatchTowr published technical analysis and a detection artifact generator for CVE-2026-50751, an authentication bypass in Check Point Remote Access and Mobile Access VPN that the vendor confirmed was actively exploited and patched on June 8; broader opportunistic attacks are now likely. Separately, phpBB forum software fixed a 10-year-old authentication bypass that let attackers log in as any user, including administrators. Google's Chrome 149 update resolved 28 vulnerabilities, including a dozen use-after-free bugs. Administrators should patch Check Point gateways, phpBB installs, and Chrome promptly given confirmed or trivial exploitation paths.

sources.title: Researchers release details, PoC for exploited Check Point VPN flaw (CVE-2026-50751)
phpBB forum fixes auth bypass bug lurking for a decade
Chrome 149 Update Patches 28 Vulnerabilities

business.text: PennDOT announced $25 million for new electric-vehicle charging stations across Western Pennsylvania.

sources.title: PennDOT announces $25 million for new EV charging stations in Western Pa.

business.text: Pittsburgh landed a $250,000 grant to upgrade Smithfield Street downtown, where the Urban Redevelopment Authority has hired an architect to transform boarded-up storefronts below the park.

sources.title: Pittsburgh receives $250K grant to use for upgrades to Smithfield Street

around_town.text: Mark your calendar: the Parkway East's Commercial Street Bridge will close for a 25-day replacement project starting July 10 at 9 p.m., shutting the stretch from the Squirrel Hill Tunnel to the Edgewood-Swissvale exit. PennDOT says it is working to minimize the disruption.

sources.title: Parkway East will close in 1 month for Commercial Street Bridge replacement project

around_town.text: City officials want to open a 110-year-old time capsule placed in the City-County Building under Mayor Joseph Armstrong in 1916, but they cannot figure out where it is hidden.

sources.title: Pittsburgh wants to open a time capsule hidden in the City-County Building. But no one can find it.

around_town.text: The demolition timeline for the Monroeville Mall remains uncertain, with no formal plans yet submitted, according to the borough's mayor.

sources.title: Monroeville Mall demolition timeline up in the air, according to mayor

around_town.text: Mister Rogers' Neighborhood now has its first dedicated YouTube channel, offering full episodes, livestreams, clips, and compilations of the Pittsburgh-made classic.

sources.title: The first-ever dedicated Mister Rogers' Neighborhood YouTube channel launches globally

events.text: The Three Rivers Arts Festival heads into its final weekend in the Cultural District, with vendors praising the festival's new home at Arts Landing.

sources.title: Vendors approve of new location of Three Rivers Arts Festival

events.text: The Holy Cross Greek Festival is back at Holy Cross Greek Orthodox Church in Mt. Lebanon, with traditional food on offer.

sources.title: The Holy Cross Greek Festival is back

events.text: Butler County is hosting a weekend Jeep celebration honoring the Bantam Jeep, which was designed locally and credited with helping win World War II.

sources.title: 'A family affair': Butler County residents celebrate history of Jeeps in their community

events.text: The North Park Swimming Pool is set to open for the season on Saturday after a mechanical issue delayed its start.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 1)
reading.summary: Zitron argues the current tech era is ending, pointing to OpenAI and Anthropic filing to go public as a scramble for exit liquidity by companies burning billions with no clear path to profitability.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Fable 5, Anthropic Alignment, AI Tiers
reading.summary: Thompson examines Fable 5 as the public release of Mythos, calling it very capable but warning that it establishes some troubling new precedents around alignment and model tiers.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: The iPhone's Last Stand
reading.summary: Thompson contends Apple's revamped Siri isn't state of the art, but as long as it works it is good enough to satisfy the consumer market.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Why Isn't AI Taking Our Jobs?
reading.summary: Newport questions the AI-industry analogy to industrial automation, exploring why predictions of AI broadly eliminating jobs haven't materialized as promised.
